seo.title: LlamaIndex Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for LlamaIndex agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams using LlamaIndex for RAG applications face heightened risks due to the critical nature of accurate case law retrieval and synthesis. Hallucinated citations or irrelevant context can lead to malpractice suits, as seen in the Mata v. Avianca case. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify outputs against legal compliance standards before deployment.

complianceRequirements.description: Systems must prevent disclosure of confidential client information and ensure retrieved context respects privilege boundaries.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies that responses contain no privileged data leakage and flags irrelevant privileged context retrievals.

complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated legal content must be clearly marked as such and reviewed by licensed attorneys before use.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex adds compliance watermarks and flags outputs requiring attorney review based on complexity thresholds.

complianceRequirements.description: All citations must reference real, verifiable cases with accurate context from source documents.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex cross-references citations against retrieved documents and flags hallucinations or fabricated case law.

complianceRequirements.description: Retrieved documents must maintain chain of custody and relevance to legal queries without context loss.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates chunk boundaries and ensures critical context continuity in retrieval chains.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated case citations
industryRisks.description: AI fabricates non-existent case law leading to court sanctions
industryRisks.example: Mata v. Avianca: $5K sanction for citing 6 fake cases

industryRisks.name: Privilege data leakage
industryRisks.description: Confidential client info appears in responses breaching attorney-client privilege

industryRisks.name: Outdated legal precedents
industryRisks.description: Stale index serves overturned case law causing malpractice
industryRisks.example: Citing Plessy v. Ferguson principles post-Brown v. Board

industryRisks.name: Chunk boundary context loss
industryRisks.description: Critical holding split across chunks leads to incomplete legal analysis
industryRisks.example: Contract termination clause split causing wrong advice

implementation.title: Wrap LlamaIndex Query Engine with Vex Verification
implementation.description: Protect against hallucinations and irrelevant retrievals by verifying query outputs match legal source documents.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader
from vex_sdk import verify

# Load legal documents
documents = SimpleDirectoryReader('case_law/').load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine()

# Wrap with Vex
def safe_legal_query(query):
    response = query_engine.query(query)
    verified = verify(
        response.response,
        context=response.source_nodes,
        policy='legal-compliance',
        rules=[
            'citations_match_sources',
            'no_privilege_leakage',
            'relevant_to_legal_query'
        ]
    )
    return verified

result = safe_legal_query('What is the holding in Miranda v. Arizona?')

implementation.title: Index Staleness Monitor for Legal Updates
implementation.description: Detect outdated case law in index and flag responses using superseded precedents.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import StorageContext, load_index_from_storage
from vex_sdk import verify
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

# Load existing index
storage_context = StorageContext.from_defaults(persist_dir='./legal_index')
index = load_index_from_storage(storage_context)

# Check index freshness
def verify_legal_freshness(query_engine, query):
    response = query_engine.query(query)
    
    # Vex freshness check
    freshness_check = verify(
        response.response,
        metadata={
            'index_last_updated': index.storage_context.persist_dir,
            'required_freshness': '30d'
        },
        rules=[
            'cases_not_overturned',
            'statutes_current',
            'index_age_acceptable'
        ],
        policy='legal-timeliness'
    )
    
    if freshness_check.flagged:
        return '⚠️ REQUIRES ATTORNEY REVIEW: Potential outdated precedents'
    return response.response

caseStudyScenario.heading: LlamaIndex in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized law firm deployed LlamaIndex to accelerate case law research for their litigation team. They indexed 50,000+ federal and state cases across their document management system, enabling associates to query precedents 10x faster than manual Westlaw searches. The system handled queries like 'precedents for motion to dismiss under Rule 12(b)(6)' and synthesized holdings from multiple cases.

However, without compliance guardrails, the system began surfacing issues: chunk boundaries split critical holdings, causing incomplete analysis; the index grew stale after quarterly updates stopped; and the synthesizer occasionally hallucinated citations resembling real cases but with fabricated facts. Junior associates unknowingly relied on these outputs for court filings.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex verification around their LlamaIndex query engine, the firm achieved 99.7% compliance on legal outputs. Vex flagged 12 hallucinated citations in the first month, caught a privilege leak from an internal memo, and enforced attorney review on 18% of complex queries. The system now watermarks all AI outputs and maintains verifiable audit trails, preventing Mata v. Avianca-style disasters while maintaining 8x research speed gains.
